payload.name: step_1_claim_parts
payload.description: Atomic components of the statement that must be checked against the table.

payload.name: step_2_evidence_mapping
payload.description: Rows, cells, and values mapped to each claim component.

payload.name: step_3_component_decisions
payload.description: Per-component labels: supported, contradicted, or not found.

payload.name: step_4_decision_rule
payload.description: How the component decisions determine the final True/False verdict.

payload.content: Follow the numbered procedure fields in order before writing `verdict`: (1) In `step_1_claim_parts`, split the statement into atomic components, where each component contains one table-checkable relation, comparison, count, date, entity, or attribute. (2) In `step_2_evidence_mapping`, map each component to specific table row(s), column(s), and cell value(s); mark evidence as missing when no supporting row or cell exists. (3) In `step_3_component_decisions`, label every component as supported, contradicted, or not found; contradicted evidence overrides partial support. (4) In `step_4_decision_rule`, apply this policy: the statement is True only if every required component is supported and no component is contradicted; otherwise it is False.
Do not skip any numbered procedure field, even for simple statements.
Keep `verdict` to the final label only: True or False.

rationale: Pilot scaffold package for TabFact procedure reasoning. Dedicated aux fields create schema pressure for claim decomposition, evidence mapping, component decisions, and final decision policy while preserving direct `verdict` scoring.
